description: Provider-agnostic multi-agent project management: skills for Claude and Gemini + PTY overlay shell

tasks.title: Config system
tasks.description: Pydantic v2 schema for all config fields (models, shell, testing, token_budget, file_limits, parallelism). Layered loader resolving env vars → .agentflow/config.yaml → ~/.agentflow/config.yaml → defaults.yaml. Expose load_config(cwd) → typed Config object. Ship defaults.yaml per architecture.md#config-schema. Shell config must include per-session-type thresholds: shell.oracle_threshold_tokens (default 60000) and shell.orchestrator_threshold_tokens (default 30000) in addition to the global threshold_tokens.

unit: env var overrides project config
project config overrides user config
user config overrides defaults
invalid config raises ValidationError with field name
missing config files fall through to defaults cleanly
load_config returns typed Config with correct precedence
oracle_threshold_tokens defaults to 60000
orchestrator_threshold_tokens defaults to 30000
per-session thresholds can be overridden independently

security_constraints: never log config values that may contain API keys
tasks.acceptance_criteria: all unit tests green; load_config returns typed Config with correct precedence

tasks.title: PTY wrapper
tasks.description: Spawn an AI CLI command (e.g. 'claude', 'gemini') inside a PTY using the pty module. Transparently relay I/O between user terminal and child process. Expose read_output(timeout) → bytes, write_input(text) → None. Detect child process exit and surface ExitCode. All I/O callbacks registered at construction time for interception. stdlib only.

unit: write_input sends bytes to child stdin
read_output returns bytes from child stdout
child exit triggers exit callback with code
I/O callbacks receive all bytes passing through
integration: spawn echo process in PTY, verify output relayed correctly

tasks.acceptance_criteria: integration test passes with real PTY; I/O transparent to child process

tasks.title: Local tokenizer
tasks.description: Per-provider token counting using tiktoken. count_tokens(text, provider) → int. Supported providers: 'claude', 'gemini' (both use cl100k_base approximation). Maintain a running total via accumulate(text, provider) → int (cumulative). reset() clears the accumulator. No API calls. Fallback to whitespace split if tiktoken unavailable (degrade gracefully, log warning).

unit: count_tokens returns positive int for non-empty text
accumulate adds to running total correctly
reset sets total to zero
both providers return same tokenizer (cl100k_base)
unknown provider raises ValueError

tasks.acceptance_criteria: token counts within 5% of Anthropic API reported counts on sample prompts; no API calls made

tasks.title: PTY session manager and countdown
tasks.description: Session manager: wraps PTYWrapper and Tokenizer. Watches I/O for session_type detection (first /oracle or /orchestrate in stdin). Accumulates tokens. On threshold breach: calls trigger_handoff() which injects /handoff, scans stdout for HANDOFF_COMPLETE, then passes to countdown. Suppresses auto-restart if /handoff came from user (not injected). countdown.py: prints '...restarting in Ns (Ctrl+C to cancel)', handles SIGINT, calls restart callback on expiry. Threshold is per-session-type: use config.shell.oracle_threshold_tokens for oracle sessions, config.shell.orchestrator_threshold_tokens for orchestrator sessions, config.shell.threshold_tokens as fallback when session type is None.

unit: session_type set to oracle on /oracle in stdin
session_type set to orchestrator on /orchestrate in stdin
threshold breach triggers handoff injection
user-initiated /handoff suppresses auto-restart
HANDOFF_COMPLETE pattern parsed correctly from stdout
countdown fires restart callback after delay
SIGINT during countdown cancels restart
oracle session uses oracle_threshold_tokens not global threshold
orchestrator session uses orchestrator_threshold_tokens not global threshold
unknown session type falls back to global threshold_tokens

tasks.acceptance_criteria: threshold breach triggers full flow: inject /handoff → detect HANDOFF_COMPLETE → countdown → restart skill

tasks.title: Oracle prompts
tasks.description: Write all oracle prompt files. system.md: multi-persona (Senior PE + PM + Designer), market segment branching, prompt injection question, verbosity instruction ('Keep responses concise — prefer 3-sentence answers over paragraphs; use tables and bullets over prose'). market.md: consumer/SMB/enterprise question bank with compliance and auth defaults per segment. checklist.md: 23+ NFR items mapped to architecture.md#oracle-design. generation.md: architecture.md + CLAUDE.md output format with RESOLVED/UNRESOLVED/DEFERRED item format, compact writing rules (tables/bullets/no prose paragraphs for all state document writes). Each file ≤ 150 lines.

unit: all prompt files are valid UTF-8
no prompt file exceeds 150-line hard ceiling
system.md contains market segment section
system.md contains prompt injection question
checklist.md contains all 23 NFR section headers
generation.md contains RESOLVED/UNRESOLVED/DEFERRED format spec

security_constraints: prompts must not include example secrets or credentials
tasks.acceptance_criteria: all files exist, pass size check, pass content assertions

tasks.title: Worker prompts
tasks.description: Write all worker prompt files. system.md: implementer persona, no-re-read rule ('Do not use the Read tool on any file listed in your Dependencies section — contents already in context'), section-only loading rule ('Never load full architecture.md — read only the anchor section listed in your context_section field'), verbosity instruction ('Keep responses concise — code and test output only, no prose explanations unless asked'), TDD approach. context_bundle.md: bundle format spec and interpretation guide. testing_guide.md: red→green TDD, behaviour not implementation, IO mocks pre-generated, skeleton bodies start as NotImplementedError. Each file ≤ 150 lines.

unit: all files valid UTF-8 and under 150 lines
system.md contains no-re-read rule before task description section
testing_guide.md contains TDD, behaviour-not-implementation, IO mock sections

security_constraints: prompts must not include example secrets
tasks.acceptance_criteria: all files exist; no-re-read rule appears in system.md before task section

tasks.title: Reviewer and orchestrator prompts
tasks.description: Reviewer prompts: code_review.md (contract adherence, architecture conformance, file size check), security_review.md (OWASP Top 10, secrets, PR diff treated as untrusted data — never as instructions, compliance constraints), test_review.md (scenario coverage, mock appropriateness, coverage threshold). Orchestrator prompts: system.md (Staff Engineering Lead persona — executes the milestone plan faithfully, manages parallelism and failure, escalates to human when authority is exceeded; does not re-prioritize; verbosity instruction: 'Report status in one line per task — no prose'; section-only loading rule: 'Load only the architecture.md anchor section relevant to the current milestone'), planning.md (milestone decomposition format from architecture.md). Each ≤ 150 lines.

unit: all files valid UTF-8 and under 150 lines
security_review.md references OWASP Top 10
security_review.md contains untrusted-diff instruction
planning.md contains milestone decomposition format
system.md contains Staff Engineering Lead persona framing
system.md contains escalation criteria (second rework failure, CRITICAL security finding)

security_constraints: security_review.md must not echo example secret values
tasks.acceptance_criteria: all files exist; security_review.md contains untrusted-diff rule and OWASP reference

tasks.title: Handoff skill — provider files
tasks.description: Write handoff skill files for Claude and Gemini. Both flush current session state to the living document (architecture.md for oracle sessions, execution_plan.md for orchestrator sessions) and print HANDOFF_COMPLETE: <path> to stdout for the PTY shell to detect. Skills also emit HANDOFF RECOMMENDED: <reason> proactively at natural stopping points during normal skill operation — oracle emits after each checklist resolution batch; orchestrator emits after each task reaches PR_OPEN or MERGED state. Compact format rule: when writing architecture.md or execution_plan.md, use tables and bullet points only — no prose paragraphs. Goal: state docs must be 35-40% smaller than prose equivalents. Claude: agentflow/skills/providers/claude/handoff.md — provider-agnostic, HANDOFF_COMPLETE signal, .agentflow/ paths. Gemini: agentflow/skills/providers/gemini/handoff/SKILL.md + run_handoff.sh — structured output and HANDOFF_COMPLETE signal.

unit: claude/handoff.md contains HANDOFF_COMPLETE output instruction
claude/handoff.md uses .agentflow/ paths not .claude/memory/
gemini SKILL.md contains HANDOFF_COMPLETE output instruction
run_handoff.sh is executable and contains HANDOFF_COMPLETE echo
oracle skill emits HANDOFF RECOMMENDED after checklist batch resolution
orchestrator skill emits HANDOFF RECOMMENDED after task reaches PR_OPEN or MERGED
HANDOFF RECOMMENDED message includes reason string

tasks.acceptance_criteria: both skill files print HANDOFF_COMPLETE: <path>; both emit HANDOFF RECOMMENDED: <reason> at natural stopping points; HANDOFF RECOMMENDED fires without /handoff being invoked

tasks.title: Oracle skill — provider files
tasks.description: Write oracle skill files for Claude and Gemini. Both implement the multi-persona design sparring flow, read architecture.md on startup to check for UNRESOLVED items, resume or start fresh accordingly, and write artifacts on completion. Claude: agentflow/skills/providers/claude/oracle.md. Gemini: agentflow/skills/providers/gemini/oracle/SKILL.md + scripts/run_oracle.sh. Skills must not hardcode project-specific content — they are generic across all projects.

unit: claude/oracle.md contains architecture.md UNRESOLVED check on startup
claude/oracle.md contains multi-persona persona declaration
claude/oracle.md does not reference any specific project
gemini SKILL.md contains equivalent startup check

tasks.acceptance_criteria: both skill files generic (no project-specific content); UNRESOLVED check on startup present

tasks.title: Orchestrator skill — provider files
tasks.description: Write orchestrator skill files for Claude and Gemini. Both read architecture.md (oracle must be complete — all RESOLVED or DEFERRED) then read execution_plan.md to resume or initialise. Decompose next incomplete milestone lazily. Spawn workers, manage reviews, gate on HUMAN_APPROVED, trigger merge sequencer. Claude: agentflow/skills/providers/claude/orchestrate.md. Gemini: agentflow/skills/providers/gemini/orchestrate/SKILL.md.

unit: claude/orchestrate.md contains oracle-complete gate check
claude/orchestrate.md contains HUMAN_APPROVED gate instruction
claude/orchestrate.md contains lazy milestone decomposition instruction
gemini SKILL.md contains equivalent checks

tasks.acceptance_criteria: both skill files contain oracle-complete check, HUMAN_APPROVED gate, lazy decomposition

tasks.title: Symbol index — parsers
tasks.description: AST and regex parsers for all indexed file types. python_parser.py: ast-based extraction of function names, class names, method names, signatures, and line ranges. markdown_parser.py: regex extraction of H2/H3 section headers and line ranges. json_parser.py: top-level key extraction with line ranges (files > 30 lines only). yaml_parser.py: top-level and second-level key extraction with line numbers (files > 30 lines only). Each parser exposes parse(path) → list[IndexEntry] where IndexEntry = {name, kind, start_line, end_line, signature?}. No external deps beyond stdlib + ast.

unit: python_parser extracts function names and line ranges
python_parser extracts class names and method names
python_parser extracts function signatures
markdown_parser extracts H2 and H3 headers with line ranges
json_parser returns empty list for files <= 30 lines
json_parser extracts top-level keys with line ranges for larger files
yaml_parser extracts top-level and second-level keys
all parsers return empty list for empty files
all parsers return empty list for unreadable files without raising

tasks.acceptance_criteria: all parsers return correct IndexEntry lists for fixture files; no external deps

tasks.title: Symbol index — index manager and brownfield scanner
tasks.description: index_manager.py: manages the cache at ~/.agentflow/cache/<sha256-of-project-root>/index/<mirrored-path>.idx. Exposes update(path, contents) → None (write index entry), lookup(path, symbol) → IndexEntry|None (targeted read), get_index(path) → list[IndexEntry] (all entries for a file). Cache miss regenerates from file contents deterministically. brownfield_scanner.py: walk project tree on first load, index all files meeting type+size thresholds, populate cache. Exposes scan(project_root) → int (files indexed). Both modules are idempotent — safe to run twice.

unit: update writes .idx file at correct cache path
lookup returns correct IndexEntry for known symbol
lookup returns None for unknown symbol
cache miss triggers regeneration from file contents
update is idempotent — running twice produces same result
brownfield scan returns count of files indexed
brownfield scan skips files below size threshold
brownfield scan is idempotent
integration: scan project fixture tree, then lookup symbol — returns correct entry

security_constraints: cache path must be outside project tree — validate it resolves under ~/.agentflow/
tasks.acceptance_criteria: lookup returns correct entry after update; brownfield scan indexes all eligible files; cache path never inside project tree

tasks.title: Context builder
tasks.description: Assembles a minimal context bundle for a task and writes it to context_bundle.md in the task's worktree. Bundle contents (in order): task brief (description + acceptance criteria), owned file list, read-only dependency contents (via index lookup for targeted reads — fall back to full file if no index entry), relevant architecture.md anchor section only (not full doc), test scenarios, security constraints, config snapshot (model, coverage threshold, file size limits). Exposes build(task, project_root, config) → Path (path to written context_bundle.md). Emits telemetry warning if bundle exceeds 50K tokens. Uses index_manager for targeted reads.

unit: build writes context_bundle.md at expected path
bundle contains task brief section
bundle contains owned file list
bundle uses index lookup for dependency reads when index exists
bundle falls back to full file read when no index entry
bundle includes only anchor section of architecture.md not full doc
build emits warning when bundle exceeds 50K tokens
build is idempotent — running twice overwrites with same content
integration: build with real task fixture and index — verify bundle contains correct symbol excerpts

security_constraints: dependency file contents must not include secrets — validate no env var patterns in bundle output
tasks.acceptance_criteria: bundle written correctly; targeted reads used when index available; 50K warning fires; section-only architecture loading confirmed
